title: County Of San Diego Instructional Service Guide Ssd Commercial
text:
KEEP A LID ON STORMWATER POLLUTION
•	Place all trash, recyclables and organics inside the appropriate container.
•	Avoid overfilling containers. Trash, recyclables and organics must be loaded even 
with the top of the container so the lids close completely. Lid damage to collection 
equipment or surrounding property can occur when overfilled containers are being 
dumped. 
•	Please DO NOT place tires, hazardous waste, or electronic waste into your 
containers.
•	Be cautious when placing heavy material, such as construction debris in your 
container. Heavy debris can quickly exceed the weight capacity of a container 
before it is even half full. Construction debris should be diverted. 
•	Please ensure that vehicles and equipment are not blocking access to your 
containers on your scheduled service day. Unobstructed access to containers is 
absolutely necessary. 
•	Rather than throwing items away in the trash, donate unwanted items to charity, friends or family.
•	Reuse glass jars, cups, tubs, aluminum foil, boxes, bags and packaging material.
•	Use cloth napkins, tablecloths, and dish towels instead of disposable items. 
•	Purchase items with less packaging by buying in bulk.
•	Bring your own coffee cup, water bottle, reusable bags, and boxes when shopping or out and 
about, rather than using disposables. 
•	Subscribe to paperless invoices and notices.
•	Opt out of junk mail. Remove your name from mailing lists by visiting OptOutPrescreen.com 
or by calling toll-free 1-888-5-OPT-OUT (1-888-567-8688) or by visiting sandiegocounty.gov 
referencing junk mail.  
WASTE REDUCTION TIPS:
